response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with strong web/backend skills, but the role explicitly requires 8 years of C++ and 5 years building large-scale infrastructure or distributed systems at compute-platform scale. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (~6 months in 2014-2015) and no demonstrated cloud-compute-platform or infrastructure-leadership experience. Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which does not map to the C++ systems requirement.
strengths: Long, varied senior engineering career with mentoring, workshops, and open-source leadership
Solid distributed/event-driven systems exposure (Sociomantic high-throughput services, GISCollective on Kubernetes)
Demonstrated technical-leadership behaviors: founding projects, running courses, driving TDD/CI/CD adoption
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ minimum — only one short C/C++ role nearly a decade ago
No production cloud-compute platform or hardware/storage architecture experience to match the infrastructure requirement
No formal tech-lead title managing cloud/DevOps engineers; leadership is informal/community-based

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform / large-scale infra ownership
Senior engineering breadth and mentoring track record
